  "text": "The gRPC v1.68.0 codebase implements comprehensive health checking and keepalive mechanisms across multiple layers:\n\n**Health Checking Service (src/cpp/server/health/):**\nThe DefaultHealthCheckService (default_health_check_service.h/cc) is the primary server-side health check implementation. It provides a HealthCheckServiceImpl that extends Service and implements the gRPC Health Checking Protocol. The WatchReactor inner class handles streaming health status updates to clients via the Watch RPC. The service maintains a map of services and their current status, notifying registered watchers when status changes occur.\n\n**Client-Side Health Checking (src/core/load_balancing/):**\nThe health_check_client.cc/h provides the MakeHealthCheckWatcher() function that enables Load Balancing policies to integrate health check data from Health.Watch streams. The HealthProducer::HealthChecker class (internal implementation) manages the health check stream lifecycle and updates connectivity state watchers based on health check responses.\n\n**Keepalive Protocol Implementation (src/core/ext/transport/chttp2/transport/):**\nThe keepalive mechanism is implemented through three policy classes:\n- Chttp2PingRatePolicy (ping_rate_policy.h/cc) controls the rate at which pings are sent, preventing ping flooding. It uses RequestSendPing() to decide when pings are allowed.\n- Chttp2PingAbusePolicy (ping_abuse_policy.h/cc) enforces server-side protection against ping abuse by tracking received pings and enforcing strike-based limits via ReceivedOnePing().\n- Chttp2PingCallbacks (ping_callbacks.h/cc) manages the lifecycle of individual pings, including timeouts and acknowledgments.\n\nThe chttp2_transport.h/cc contains the grpc_chttp2_keepalive_state enum (WAITING, PINGING, DYING, DISABLED) defined in internal.h, which manages the transport-level keepalive state machine. The writing.cc file implements the actual keepalive ping sending logic.\n\n**Connectivity State Tracking (src/core/lib/transport/):**\nConnectivityStateWatcherInterface (connectivity_state.h/cc) defines the watcher pattern for monitoring connectivity changes. AsyncConnectivityStateWatcherInterface provides asynchronous notifications. ConnectivityStateTracker maintains the current state and notifies all registered watchers of state transitions.\n\n**Channel-Level Watchers (src/core/client_channel/):**\nClientChannelFilter (client_channel_filter.h/cc) implements the client channel filter and contains ExternalConnectivityWatcher to handle external connectivity watching. The Subchannel class (subchannel.h) provides StartWatch() to register ConnectivityStateWatcher implementations and manage the subchannel's connectivity state.\n\n**Channel Idle Management (src/core/ext/filters/channel_idle/):**\nLegacyChannelIdleFilter (legacy_channel_idle_filter.h/cc) implements channel-level connectivity watching via its ConnectivityWatcher inner class, which derives from AsyncConnectivityStateWatcherInterface to track channel activity and enforce idle timeouts.\n\nThe integration allows health checks to influence connectivity state, which triggers watchers throughout the stack, ultimately driving connection management and keepalive decisions."

## Summary of Findings

I've identified and documented all the key C++ source and header files implementing gRPC's health checking service and keepalive mechanism:

**Health Checking Service (src/cpp/server/health/)**
- `DefaultHealthCheckService`: Main server-side implementation
- `HealthCheckServiceImpl`: Service implementation with Watch stream handling
- `WatchReactor`: Manages streaming health status updates to clients

**Keepalive Protocol (src/core/ext/transport/chttp2/transport/)**
- `Chttp2PingRatePolicy`: Controls ping sending rates and prevents flood attacks
- `Chttp2PingAbusePolicy`: Server-side protection against ping abuse with strike-based limits
- `Chttp2PingCallbacks`: Manages individual ping lifecycle and timeouts
- `chttp2_transport`: Contains keepalive state machine (WAITING, PINGING, DYING, DISABLED)

**Client-Side Health Checking (src/core/load_balancing/)**
- `MakeHealthCheckWatcher()`: Creates health check watchers for load balancers
- `HealthProducer::HealthChecker`: Manages health check stream lifecycle

**Connectivity State Management (src/core/lib/transport/)**
- `ConnectivityStateWatcherInterface`: Base watcher pattern
- `AsyncConnectivityStateWatcherInterface`: Async notification support
- `ConnectivityStateTracker`: Tracks and notifies state changes

**Channel-Level Implementation**
- `ClientChannelFilter`: Client channel filter with external connectivity watching
- `LegacyChannelIdleFilter`: Channel idle timeout management with connectivity watching
- `Subchannel`: Subchannel connectivity watching via `StartWatch()`

    40→// Interface for watching connectivity state.
    41→// Subclasses must implement the Notify() method.
    42→//
    43→// Note: Most callers will want to use
    44→// AsyncConnectivityStateWatcherInterface instead.
    45→class ConnectivityStateWatcherInterface
    46→    : public InternallyRefCounted<ConnectivityStateWatcherInterface> {
    47→ public:
    48→  ~ConnectivityStateWatcherInterface() override = default;
    49→
    50→  // Notifies the watcher that the state has changed to new_state.
    51→  virtual void Notify(grpc_connectivity_state new_state,
    52→                      const absl::Status& status) = 0;
    53→
    54→  void Orphan() override { Unref(); }
    55→};
    56→
    57→// An alternative watcher interface that performs notifications via an
    58→// asynchronous callback scheduled on the ExecCtx.
    59→// Subclasses must implement the OnConnectivityStateChange() method.
    60→class AsyncConnectivityStateWatcherInterface
    61→    : public ConnectivityStateWatcherInterface {
    62→ public:
    63→  ~AsyncConnectivityStateWatcherInterface() override = default;
    64→
    65→  // Schedules a closure on the ExecCtx to invoke
    66→  // OnConnectivityStateChange() asynchronously.
    67→  void Notify(grpc_connectivity_state new_state,
    68→              const absl::Status& status) final;
    69→
    70→ protected:
    71→  class Notifier;
    72→
    73→  // If \a work_serializer is nullptr, then the notification will be scheduled
    74→  // on the ExecCtx.
    75→  explicit AsyncConnectivityStateWatcherInterface(
    76→      std::shared_ptr<WorkSerializer> work_serializer = nullptr)
    77→      : work_serializer_(std::move(work_serializer)) {}
    78→
    79→  // Invoked asynchronously when Notify() is called.
    80→  virtual void OnConnectivityStateChange(grpc_connectivity_state new_state,
    81→                                         const absl::Status& status) = 0;
    82→
    83→ private:
    84→  std::shared_ptr<WorkSerializer> work_serializer_;
    85→};

    87→// Tracks connectivity state.  Maintains a list of watchers that are
    88→// notified whenever the state changes.
    89→//
    90→// Note that once the state becomes SHUTDOWN, watchers will be notified
    91→// and then automatically orphaned (i.e., RemoveWatcher() does not need
    92→// to be called).
    93→class ConnectivityStateTracker {
    94→ public:
    95→  explicit ConnectivityStateTracker(
    96→      const char* name, grpc_connectivity_state state = GRPC_CHANNEL_IDLE,
    97→      const absl::Status& status = absl::Status())
    98→      : name_(name), state_(state), status_(status) {}
    99→
   100→  ~ConnectivityStateTracker();
   101→
   102→  // Adds a watcher.
   103→  // If the current state is different than initial_state, the watcher
   104→  // will be notified immediately.  Otherwise, it will be notified
   105→  // whenever the state changes.
   106→  // Not thread safe; access must be serialized with an external lock.
   107→  void AddWatcher(grpc_connectivity_state initial_state,
   108→                  OrphanablePtr<ConnectivityStateWatcherInterface> watcher);
   109→
   110→  // Removes a watcher.  The watcher will be orphaned.
   111→  // Not thread safe; access must be serialized with an external lock.
   112→  void RemoveWatcher(ConnectivityStateWatcherInterface* watcher);
   113→
   114→  // Sets connectivity state.
   115→  // Not thread safe; access must be serialized with an external lock.
   116→  void SetState(grpc_connectivity_state state, const absl::Status& status,
   117→                const char* reason);
   118→
   119→  // Gets the current state.
   120→  // Thread safe; no need to use an external lock.
   121→  grpc_connectivity_state state() const;
   122→
   123→  // Get the current status.
   124→  // Not thread safe; access must be serialized with an external lock.
   125→  absl::Status status() const { return status_; }
   126→
   127→  // Returns the number of watchers.
   128→  // Not thread safe; access must be serialized with an external lock.
   129→  size_t NumWatchers() const { return watchers_.size(); }
   130→
   131→ private:
   132→  const char* name_;
   133→  std::atomic<grpc_connectivity_state> state_{grpc_connectivity_state()};
   134→  absl::Status status_;
   135→  // TODO(roth): Once we can use C++-14 heterogeneous lookups, this can
   136→  // be a set instead of a map.
   137→  std::map<ConnectivityStateWatcherInterface*,
   138→           OrphanablePtr<ConnectivityStateWatcherInterface>>
   139→      watchers_;
   140→};
   141→
   142→}  // namespace grpc_core
   143→
   144→#endif  // GRPC_SRC_CORE_LIB_TRANSPORT_CONNECTIVITY_STATE_H

    31→// The data is reported from via a Health.Watch stream established on the
    32→// subchannel whenever an LB policy registers a watcher.
    33→//

    35→// SubchannelInterface::ConnectivityStateWatcherInterface, which will
    36→// receive connectivity state updates with health check status taken
    37→// into account.  It will then register that watcher with the subchannel
    38→// like this:
    39→//   subchannel->AddDataWatcher(
    40→//       MakeHealthCheckWatcher(
    41→//           work_serializer(), channel_args,
    42→//           std::make_unique<MyConnectivityStateWatcherSubclass>(...)));
    43→
    44→std::unique_ptr<SubchannelInterface::DataWatcherInterface>
    45→MakeHealthCheckWatcher(
    46→    std::shared_ptr<WorkSerializer> work_serializer, const ChannelArgs& args,
    47→    std::unique_ptr<SubchannelInterface::ConnectivityStateWatcherInterface>
    48→        watcher);
    49→
    50→}  // namespace grpc_core

  "text": "The gRPC v1.68.0 codebase implements comprehensive health checking and keepalive mechanisms across multiple layers:\n\n**Health Checking Service (src/cpp/server/health/):**\nThe DefaultHealthCheckService (default_health_check_service.h/cc) is the primary server-side health check implementation. It provides a HealthCheckServiceImpl that extends Service and implements the gRPC Health Checking Protocol. The WatchReactor inner class handles streaming health status updates to clients via the Watch RPC. The service maintains a map of services and their current status, notifying registered watchers when status changes occur.\n\n**Client-Side Health Checking (src/core/load_balancing/):**\nThe health_check_client.cc/h provides the MakeHealthCheckWatcher() function that enables Load Balancing policies to integrate health check data from Health.Watch streams. The HealthProducer::HealthChecker class (internal implementation) manages the health check stream lifecycle and updates connectivity state watchers based on health check responses.\n\n**Keepalive Protocol Implementation (src/core/ext/transport/chttp2/transport/):**\nThe keepalive mechanism is implemented through three policy classes:\n- Chttp2PingRatePolicy (ping_rate_policy.h/cc) controls the rate at which pings are sent, preventing ping flooding. It uses RequestSendPing() to decide when pings are allowed.\n- Chttp2PingAbusePolicy (ping_abuse_policy.h/cc) enforces server-side protection against ping abuse by tracking received pings and enforcing strike-based limits via ReceivedOnePing().\n- Chttp2PingCallbacks (ping_callbacks.h/cc) manages the lifecycle of individual pings, including timeouts and acknowledgments.\n\nThe chttp2_transport.h/cc contains the grpc_chttp2_keepalive_state enum (WAITING, PINGING, DYING, DISABLED) defined in internal.h, which manages the transport-level keepalive state machine. The writing.cc file implements the actual keepalive ping sending logic.\n\n**Connectivity State Tracking (src/core/lib/transport/):**\nConnectivityStateWatcherInterface (connectivity_state.h/cc) defines the watcher pattern for monitoring connectivity changes. AsyncConnectivityStateWatcherInterface provides asynchronous notifications.
